metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for the POSITIVE claim in AC1/AC3 — e.g. `grep -niE 'judgment|genuinely improve' CLAUDE.md examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` must return at least one hit — so the presence of judgment-based replacement language is verified, not only the absence of the old mandate.
findings.finding: AC1 and AC3 each assert a POSITIVE property (replacement text is 'judgment-based and does not discourage acting on advisories'; SKILL.md is 'softened to judgment framing') but provide no proving command for that positive claim — only the REMOVAL of the mandate is verifiable via the grep in AC3. An agent could delete the mandate and insert neutral or discouraging text and still pass all provided commands.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('the replacement framing is judgment-based and does not discourage acting on advisories') and AC3 ('examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md is softened to judgment framing')
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: Proving command: `grep -niE 'judgment|genuinely improve|apply.*improve' CLAUDE.md` must return at least one match in the edited paragraph. Append to AC3: `grep -niE 'judgment|genuinely improve' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` must return at least one match in the softened sentence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assess whether `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` ('remediate findings before claiming') needs to be softened to judgment-based framing consistent with the CLAUDE.md change, OR explicitly justify why this unqualified phrasing does not re-impose the mandate; update the AC3 grep regex to catch this pattern if softening is required.
findings.finding: The plan's sweep scope and AC3 grep regex do not cover examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135, which contains 'remediate findings before claiming' — an unqualified echo that, unlike the named targets, does not say 'all/every valid' but could still be read by agents as requiring full remediation of all advisory findings before claiming.
findings.location: What section (sweep scope) and AC3 (proving grep); gap is at examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135
findings.suggested_fix: Change SKILL.md:135 from 'remediate findings before claiming' to 'review and apply findings before claiming' (or similar judgment framing), and broaden the AC3 grep to also check `grep -rniE 'remediate (all|every|the)? ?findings' examples/` to catch unqualified instances.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Broaden the AC3 grep pattern (or add a second command) to catch rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135's 'remediate findings before claiming' phrasing, and decide whether that line also needs softening to judgment-based framing.
findings.finding: The AC3 grep proving command ('grep -rniE "remediate (all|every) valid|must remediate all" CLAUDE.md docs/ CONTRIBUTING.md examples/') will NOT match the rebar-brainstorm echo at examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 ('remediate findings before claiming'), producing a false-negative: the AC passes green while a mandate-adjacent echo in an agent-facing skill doc remains unchecked. The plan says to sweep ALL agent-facing docs under examples/ but the verification pattern is too narrow to confirm completeness.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second proving command: `grep -rniE 'remediate' examples/claude-skills/ | grep -viE 'BLOCK|fail|retry|completion'` to surface any residual remediate-findings phrasing in skill docs, and explicitly address rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 in the What section's sweep list.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out note (e.g., 'if advisory-uptake rate drops below X% vs baseline, revert this change via git revert <sha> and re-tighten the mandate') or reference the specific rollback trigger documented in epic 9d50 AC2.
findings.finding: The plan removes a behavior that agent consumers actively rely on (the mandatory-remediate mandate) but states no explicit rollback or back-out path. The observation data lives in epic 9d50 AC2, not in this plan, leaving the remediation path for a bad outcome unspecified.
findings.location: What section (overall) and the observation note at the end of What
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence in the What section: 'Rollback: if the measurement framework (epic 9d50 AC2) signals advisory-uptake regression below the baseline threshold, revert this change with `git revert <sha>` and restore the original mandate text.'

findings.finding: The AC's grep sweep scopes only to 'CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md', but `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136` contains 'remediate every valid finding' — mandatory framing equivalent to what is being removed from CLAUDE.md — and `examples/` is outside the sweep. Agents using that skill file will still receive the mandate being loosened, defeating the mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'No other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule (grep for "remediate" + "advisory" across CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `examples/` to the AC3 grep scope: 'grep -r "remediate" -- CLAUDE.md docs/ CONTRIBUTING.md examples/' and update SKILL.md:136 to mirror the judgment-based replacement framing (e.g., 'review findings and apply the ones that genuinely improve the plan; a BLOCK must still be remediated and re-reviewed').

findings.finding: The AC grep scope ('CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md') excludes `examples/`, but `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136` contains 'remediate every valid finding' — mandatory-remediate language that directly echoes the CLAUDE.md mandate. The plan would pass its own AC3 grep without finding this echo.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC3: 'No other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule (grep for remediate + advisory across CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `examples/` to the AC3 grep: `grep -r 'remediate' examples/` and decide whether SKILL.md's mandatory framing should be updated in this same change or tracked as a follow-on.

findings.finding: The plan's AC3 grep sweep ('CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md') omits examples/claude-skills/, which contains at least two files with mandatory-advisory-remediation echoes: rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136 ('remediate every valid finding') and rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 ('remediate findings before claiming'). These are agent-facing skill guides that agents read directly and that mirror the CLAUDE.md mandate being loosened; if CLAUDE.md is relaxed but these skill files retain the mandatory framing, agents following the skill files would still loop on advisories.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `examples/claude-skills/` to the AC3 grep scope. In rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136, replace 'remediate every valid finding (fix the real ones; justify any you judge invalid)' with judgment-framed language consistent with the new CLAUDE.md text (e.g. 'review findings and apply the ones that genuinely improve the plan; BLOCKs must be remediated before re-running'). Apply the same adjustment to rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
